heading: Availability and allocation of funds; royalties from mineral deposits
label: 5207 Availability and allocation of funds; royalties from mineral deposits
text: All funds appropriated under the several grants of authority contained in the Act of () [ et seq.], are hereby made available for use under the provisions of this chapter, and Oklahoma Indians shall be accorded and allocated a fair and just share of any and all funds appropriated after , under the authorization herein set forth: , That any royalties, bonuses, or other revenues derived from mineral deposits underlying lands purchased in Oklahoma under the authority granted by this chapter, or by the Act of , shall be deposited in the Treasury of the United States, and such revenues are made available for expenditure by the Secretary of the Interior for the acquisition of lands and for loans to Indians in Oklahoma as authorized by this chapter and by the Act of ().June 18, 1934June 26, 1936June 18, 1934June 18, 193448 Stat. 98425 U.S.C. 510148 Stat. 984Provided
notes: References in TextAct of , referred to in text, popularly known as the Indian Reorganization Act, is classified generally to chapter 45 (§ 5101 et seq.) of this title. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.June 18, 1934section 5101 of this title
CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 507 of this title
